school.name: ENKA Schools
school.address: Poligon Mah. Sarıyer Cad. No:121/1, 34460 Sarıyer İstanbul

neighborhoods.name: Çamlıtepe / Hacıosman
neighborhoods.oneLiner: Calm, green, metro-connected easy landing pad
neighborhoods.desc: Residential and quiet right by the station, with good local amenities and a growing expat presence. Metro connects you to the rest of the city in minutes.

neighborhoods.name: Tarabya
neighborhoods.desc: Charming waterfront village with consulate summer residences, good restaurants and a slower pace that helps newcomers decompress. Upscale but easy to navigate.

neighborhoods.name: Kireçburnu
neighborhoods.desc: A pretty waterfront area known for its seafood restaurants.
neighborhoods.caveat: May not be the most convenient for getting to/from school.

neighborhoods.name: Darüşşafaka
neighborhoods.oneLiner: Quiet, green, walkable to the metro
neighborhoods.desc: Green and residential right by the metro stop, with less traffic than areas further south. A practical, lower-friction landing spot. Compounds like Gazeticiler Sitesi can have decent options.

neighborhoods.name: Emirgan
neighborhoods.oneLiner: Coveted Bosphorus village with a famous park
neighborhoods.desc: One of the most coveted Bosphorus addresses: water views, the famous Emirgan Park (spectacular during tulip season), fish restaurants and a relaxed village pace.
neighborhoods.caveat: Housing skews expensive.

neighborhoods.name: Maslak
neighborhoods.desc: Mainly a business center, with fancy residential compounds behind it. One of the neighborhoods the school flags as walking-distance-friendly.
neighborhoods.caveat: Few residential options in Maslak proper.

neighborhoods.name: Poligon
neighborhoods.desc: Very close to the school, with some lower-cost options.
neighborhoods.caveat: The street feel varies; check Google Street View first.

neighborhoods.name: Reşitpaşa
neighborhoods.desc: Has some of the more affordable options in the area.
neighborhoods.caveat: The neighborhood may not be what you want; check Google Street View.

neighborhoods.name: Yeniköy
neighborhoods.desc: A lovely Bosphorus neighborhood.
neighborhoods.caveat: Expensive.

neighborhoods.name: İstinye
neighborhoods.oneLiner: Walk to school; huge mall has everything
neighborhoods.desc: A Bosphorus cove anchored by the İstinye Park mall, genuinely useful when newly arrived (furniture, groceries, pharmacy, cinema). Walking distance to school, up a hill.
neighborhoods.caveat: Uphill walk to school.

neighborhoods.name: Baltalimanı
neighborhoods.desc: A tiny, protected cove between Emirgan and Rumelihisarı: a small fishing harbour and a few waterfront restaurants. Housing is mostly older villas and garden apartments.
neighborhoods.caveat: No metro; infrequent buses; retail is in Emirgan/İstinye 10-15 min away.

neighborhoods.name: Levent
neighborhoods.desc: Istanbul's most internationally legible neighborhood: dense with international restaurants, gyms and malls, with direct M2 access. Max urban convenience, zero settling-in friction.

neighborhoods.name: Nisbetiye
neighborhoods.oneLiner: Quiet, leafy, favorite of long-stay expats
neighborhoods.desc: The quieter, residential counterpart to Levent: low-rise streets, tree-lined avenues, boutique cafes and embassies. Very walkable and safe.

neighborhoods.name: Bebek
neighborhoods.desc: Arguably the single most desirable expat address on the European Bosphorus shore: legendary seafront promenade, iconic café culture, and a dense international community.
neighborhoods.caveat: Expensive.

neighborhoods.name: Etiler
neighborhoods.desc: Consistently among the city's most prestigious addresses: low-rise villas, great restaurants, top private schools nearby and a strong established expat community.

neighborhoods.name: Esentepe
neighborhoods.oneLiner: Most practical; walkable from the stop
neighborhoods.desc: Essentially walkable from Zincirlikuyu: a mixed business-residential district with modern high-rise stock, good dining and excellent connectivity. Zorlu Centre is minutes away.

neighborhoods.name: Balmumcu
neighborhoods.desc: A quiet, low-key residential pocket between Zincirlikuyu and Beşiktaş, with Zorlu Centre on its doorstep. Central access without the buzz.

neighborhoods.name: Arnavutköy
neighborhoods.desc: One of the last intact Ottoman Bosphorus villages: a waterfront row of 19th-century wooden yalıs and an unhurried pace. Popular with a creative, culturally literate expat crowd.
neighborhoods.caveat: Older, quirky housing; car or bus dependent.

neighborhoods.name: Kuruçeşme
neighborhoods.desc: A narrow Bosphorus-front strip famous for summer beach clubs and upscale meyhanes, with one of the most spectacular daily commutes in Europe.
neighborhoods.caveat: Limited, pricey housing; best for a single or couple without children.

neighborhoods.name: Kültür
neighborhoods.oneLiner: Etiler adjacency without the price tag
neighborhoods.desc: A quieter residential mahalle in the hills between Etiler and the Bosphorus ridge: calm, green, low-rise, with easy access to Etiler's cafe scene. Understated value.

neighborhoods.name: Rumelihisarı
neighborhoods.desc: Built around the 15th-century fortress, on one of the most scenic stretches of the Bosphorus, next to Boğaziçi University. Intellectual, international energy and excellent waterfront fish restaurants.
neighborhoods.caveat: Small village feel, limited retail.

neighborhoods.name: Gayrettepe
neighborhoods.desc: A transitional business/residential area well connected by both M2 metro and metrobüs, with mid-range apartments. The commute math works out better than almost anywhere else.
neighborhoods.caveat: Lacks the prestige or character of Etiler or Bebek.

neighborhoods.name: Dikilitaş
neighborhoods.desc: A compact residential mahalle between Balmumcu and Beşiktaş centre: good local shops, walkable to the Bosphorus and within reach of the ferry and metrobüs. Good value for location.

neighborhoods.name: Akatlar
neighborhoods.desc: A well-established residential district popular with expats: big supermarkets (Migros, CarrefourSA), parks and a calm suburban feel rare this close to the center.
neighborhoods.caveat: Less charismatic than the Bosphorus neighborhoods.

neighborhoods.name: Mecidiyeköy
neighborhoods.desc: One of the best-connected single points on the European side (metrobüs, M2 metro, many buses). Dense and urban, with reasonable rents for the location.
neighborhoods.caveat: Loud, lacks greenery.

neighborhoods.name: 19 Mayıs
neighborhoods.desc: Just northeast of Mecidiyeköy, quieter and more residential, with solid everyday infrastructure. Central access at mid-range rents without the chaos. Honest and unpretentious.

neighborhoods.name: İzzet Paşa
neighborhoods.desc: A small, tightly built residential mahalle with local bakeries and tea houses that make daily life easy. Central enough to matter, removed enough to breathe.

neighborhoods.name: Şişli (merkez)
neighborhoods.desc: The commercial heart of Şişli: department stores, private hospitals (good for expats), consulates and a cosmopolitan history. Excellent hospital and clinic access, mid-range rents.

neighborhoods.name: Teşvikiye & Meşrutiyet
neighborhoods.desc: The polished heart of old European Istanbul money: premium boutiques, a density of private clinics, large prestige buildings, with the Nişantaşı dining ecosystem on the doorstep.
neighborhoods.caveat: Expensive.

neighborhoods.name: Harbiye & Halaskargazi
neighborhoods.desc: A practical address cluster equidistant from Taksim, Nişantaşı and Şişli, with excellent bus and metro access. Logistics over aesthetics.

neighborhoods.name: Abbasağa & Vişnezade
neighborhoods.desc: Hillside mahalles just inland from Beşiktaş, among the most pleasant everyday-living areas in the city: a beloved park, independent cafes and wine bars. Popular with younger expats.
neighborhoods.caveat: Rents have risen but remain reasonable vs the Bosphorus corridor.

neighborhoods.name: Beşiktaş (merkez)
neighborhoods.desc: The beating heart of the district: ferry terminal, fish market, stadium and a chaotic but beloved square. Outstanding connectivity (ferry to Asia, metrobüs, buses) and a genuinely mixed population.
neighborhoods.caveat: Not quiet; stadium matchdays require adjustment.

neighborhoods.name: Cihannüma
neighborhoods.desc: Perched on the ridge above Beşiktaş, with panoramic views toward the Marmara and the Asian shore. Quiet, residential and slightly bohemian-intellectual.
neighborhoods.caveat: Walking back up from Beşiktaş counts as cardio.

neighborhoods.name: Bomonti & Feriköy
neighborhoods.desc: Adjacent gentrified neighborhoods with boutique hotels, craft beer bars, design studios and one of Istanbul's best organic markets (Feriköy). Good M2 access via Osmanbey or Şişli.

neighborhoods.name: İnönü & Gümüşsuyu
neighborhoods.desc: Steep neighborhoods cascading from Taksim toward Kabataş, with spectacular Bosphorus and Marmara views from upper floors and direct access to the Kabataş metrobüs/tram interchange.
neighborhoods.caveat: Very steep terrain; vertical, dense living.

neighborhoods.name: Cihangir
neighborhoods.desc: Istanbul's most internationally recognised bohemian neighborhood: independent cafes, bookshops, street cats and a long-established creative expat community. English is widely spoken and it absorbs newcomers gracefully.
neighborhoods.caveat: Steep hills, older buildings with variable maintenance; rents have risen sharply.
